title: Bootstrap benchmark crate and module skeleton
description:
Context:
benchmark currently has only docs. Create the initial Rust crate and file layout that matches the module skeleton in docs/PLAN_BENCHMARK.md so the swarm can work against stable paths instead of inventing structure.

Deliverables:
- Cargo.toml with pinned core dependencies and edition settings
- src/main.rs plus empty module files for cli, assertions, candidate, key_check, compare, engine, lock_check, report, render, and refusal
- tests/ directory with named test files present
- minimal compileable shell with placeholder wiring

Acceptance:
- cargo test compiles the crate shell
- every planned module path exists
- no behavioral shortcuts are taken just to make the shell compile

Suggested swarm lane:
foundation / repo bootstrap

title: Implement candidate loading for row-oriented formats
description:
Context:
Benchmark v0 only accepts one row-oriented relation. Implement format detection and DuckDB loading for CSV, JSONL, Parquet, and row-oriented JSON without guessing document semantics.

Deliverables:
- extension-based candidate format detection
- DuckDB loading path for supported formats
- row-oriented shape checks for JSON inputs
- refusal path for unsupported or document-shaped inputs

Acceptance:
- CSV, JSONL, Parquet, and row-oriented JSON all materialize deterministically
- nested or document-shaped JSON is rejected rather than flattened heuristically
- candidate loading is isolated from scoring math

Suggested swarm lane:
data access / DuckDB

title: Establish benchmark fixture corpus and named test suites
description:
Context:
The plan now names BENCH_U and BENCH_I coverage. Create the fixture layout and test file skeletons early so the rest of the swarm can land work against a shared test surface.

Deliverables:
- tests/fixtures directory layout for candidate, assertion, and lock inputs
- test files for cli, scoring_matrix, refusals, and lock_integration
- initial fixture naming conventions that map cleanly to the plan contracts and threats
- placeholders or pending tests named after BENCH_U / BENCH_I ids

Acceptance:
- the crate has a stable fixture structure before feature code spreads
- test names line up with the plan matrix
- future agents can add cases without reshaping the harness

Suggested swarm lane:
fixtures / test harness

title: Implement benchmark v0 scoring primitive
description:
Context:
benchmark now has an implementation-grade plan in docs/PLAN_BENCHMARK.md, but the repo still has no code. This epic tracks the end-to-end delivery of the benchmark.v0 scoring primitive as defined by that plan.

Scope:
- one candidate artifact materialized as one row-oriented relation
- one assertions JSONL file
- explicit compare_as semantics
- deterministic PASS/FAIL/SKIP report in human and JSON modes
- optional lock verification before scoring

Non-goals:
- document-shaped candidate evaluation
- automatic gold-set generation
- policy decisions owned by assess

Exit criteria:
- the crate exists and the D1-D9 stages in PLAN_BENCHMARK.md are satisfied
- named BENCH_U/BENCH_I tests and quality gates are green
- benchmark is ready for swarm implementation without further architectural discovery

title: Drive benchmark end-to-end through named quality gates
description:
Context:
After the feature lanes land, one issue needs to pull the implementation together, finish the BENCH_U/BENCH_I matrix, and make the quality gates from the plan actually pass.

Deliverables:
- end-to-end CLI wiring across parser, loader, compare, scoring, render, and lock paths
- completion of named unit and integration tests
- determinism proof for repeated JSON output
- green fmt, clippy, cargo test, and ubs runs

Acceptance:
- Gate 1 through Gate 6 in PLAN_BENCHMARK.md pass
- benchmark is ready for first real implementation review
- no stage-D9 cleanup is deferred into hidden follow-up work

Suggested swarm lane:
integration / hardening

title: Wire benchmark CLI surface and refusal plumbing
description:
Context:
Implement the top-level command contract from PLAN_BENCHMARK.md: arguments, exit codes, mode selection, and refusal envelope rendering. This issue should stop before DuckDB scoring behavior.

Deliverables:
- clap-based CLI parsing for candidate path, assertions path, key, lock flags, and json mode
- top-level exit code mapping for PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL
- refusal envelope structs and rendering path
- help and version output wired through main

Acceptance:
- help text matches the plan closely
- refusal mode emits the documented envelope shape
- CLI shell can be exercised without real scoring logic

Suggested swarm lane:
operator surface / CLI

title: Implement explicit comparison engine semantics
description:
Context:
The benchmark tool only works if comparison semantics are explicit and deterministic. Implement string, number, percent, and date comparisons exactly as the plan defines them.

Deliverables:
- comparison functions or traits for string, number, percent, and date
- absolute tolerance handling for number and percent only
- no implicit fallback from failed numeric parsing to string equality
- deterministic failure detail values suitable for reports

Acceptance:
- percent mode does not reinterpret ratio-form decimals
- date mode normalizes canonical date text only
- invalid tolerance usage is rejected upstream, not papered over here

Suggested swarm lane:
comparison semantics

title: Implement scoring engine and summary math
description:
Context:
This is the core evaluator: join assertions to candidate rows, produce PASS/FAIL/SKIP outcomes, and compute deterministic summary metrics without collapsing accuracy and coverage.

Deliverables:
- assertion-to-candidate join/projection evaluation path
- PASS / FAIL / SKIP_ENTITY / SKIP_FIELD outcome generation
- summary math for total, passed, failed, skipped, resolved, accuracy, coverage, and by_severity
- stable ordering of failures and skips in assertion order

Acceptance:
- missingness stays separate from failure
- accuracy is null when resolved is zero
- repeated runs over the same inputs emit the same row ordering

Suggested swarm lane:
core scoring engine

title: Implement benchmark report rendering for JSON and human modes
description:
Context:
Benchmark is a report tool. Implement the JSON contract and the default human-readable summary as two renderings of the same report object.

Deliverables:
- report structs for summary, failures, skips, and refusal payloads
- JSON serializer that matches PLAN_BENCHMARK.md
- compact human output for PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL paths
- stable field ordering and deterministic line formatting where practical

Acceptance:
- human mode never invents different counts or semantics from JSON mode
- row-level failures and skips are readable in operator mode
- refusal rendering still preserves the JSON contract as source of truth

Suggested swarm lane:
report surfaces

title: Implement assertion parsing and semantic validation
description:
Context:
Benchmark correctness starts with a trustworthy assertion contract. Implement the JSONL parser and all schema/semantic validation around entity, field, compare_as, tolerance, severity, and source.

Deliverables:
- assertion structs and serde layer
- line-by-line JSONL loader with stable ordering
- validation for compare_as defaults and legal values
- validation that tolerance is only accepted for number and percent modes
- refusal mapping for malformed or semantically invalid assertions

Acceptance:
- malformed lines identify their source line
- illegal compare_as / tolerance combinations refuse deterministically
- zero valid assertions maps to E_EMPTY_ASSERTIONS

Suggested swarm lane:
input contract / assertions

title: Implement key validation and lookup planning
description:
Context:
Benchmark refuses ambiguous lookup. Add the key existence, uniqueness, and null/blank validation queries that gate scoring before any value comparisons run.

Deliverables:
- key existence check against the loaded candidate relation
- uniqueness query for benchmarked rows
- null or blank key detection for benchmarked rows
- clear refusal detail payloads for E_KEY_NOT_FOUND, E_KEY_NOT_UNIQUE, and E_KEY_NULL

Acceptance:
- scoring never begins if key discipline fails
- duplicate and blank-key failures are distinguishable
- key validation is reusable by the score engine without duplicating SQL

Suggested swarm lane:
lookup integrity

title: Implement lock verification before scoring
description:
Context:
Benchmark optionally accepts one or more lockfiles and must verify candidate membership and hash integrity before any scoring happens. This preserves the spine boundary that benchmark evaluates trusted inputs, not drifting local files.

Deliverables:
- lockfile loading path for repeatable --lock flags
- candidate membership checks against provided lockfiles
- candidate hash verification against the matched lock entry
- input_verification report payload and refusal wiring for lock failures

Acceptance:
- non-member candidates refuse with E_INPUT_NOT_LOCKED
- hash drift refuses with E_INPUT_DRIFT
- successful lock verification is visible in the report

Suggested swarm lane:
input integrity / lock integration
